phrases.intent_en: Ask where the cheese shop is
phrases.text_local: La fromagerie, c'est de quel côté ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah fro-mah-zhuh-REE say duh kel ko-TAY
phrases.cultural_note: Marché Atwater is home to several acclaimed fromageries carrying local Québec artisan cheeses. Try Oka, Le Migneron, or Fleurmier.

responses.english: At the back of the market, on the left.
responses.phonetic: oh FOHN doo mar-SHAY ah GOHSH
responses.french: Au fond du marché, à gauche.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a recommendation for a local cheese
phrases.text_local: Quel fromage du Québec est-ce que vous recommandez ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel fro-MAHZH doo kay-BEK ess-KUH voo ruh-ko-mon-DAY
phrases.cultural_note: Québec has a thriving artisan cheese industry. Fromagers love recommending local favourites — don't hesitate to ask.

responses.english: Try the Oka, it is a classic.
responses.phonetic: eh-say-YAY luh oh-KAH say tuhn klah-SEEK
responses.french: Essayez le Oka, c'est un classique.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a half kilo of charcuterie
phrases.text_local: Je prendrais un demi-kilo de charcuterie, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh prahn-DREH un duh-MEE-kee-loh duh shar-koo-tuh-REE seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: The market's butchers carry local meats including smoked meats, sausages, and game. Excellent for a canal-side picnic.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the canal bike path nearby
phrases.text_local: La piste cyclable du canal, c'est par là ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah PEEST see-KLAHBL doo kah-NAHL say par LAH
phrases.cultural_note: The Lachine Canal bike path starts just outside the market. You can rent bikes nearby for a scenic ride toward Old Montréal.

responses.english: The bike path is right behind the market.
responses.phonetic: lah PEEST see-KLABL eh ZHOOST dair-YAIR luh mar-SHAY
responses.french: La piste cyclable est juste derrière le marché.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there is a good place to eat nearby
phrases.text_local: Y'a-tu un bon endroit pour manger pas trop loin ?
phrases.pronunciation: yah-TOO un bon on-DWAH poor mon-ZHAY pah troh LWAH
phrases.cultural_note: This phrasing uses Québécois informal speech ('y'a-tu' for 'est-ce qu'il y a'). Locals will appreciate it and point you to great spots.

responses.english: There are good restaurants on the canal.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah duh bohn res-toh-RAHN soor luh kah-NAHL
responses.french: Il y a de bons restaurants sur le canal.
